headline: Quanta vs. Wolters Kluwer: The AI themes to follow on these stocks
summary: In the latest installment of Yahoo Finance's Good Buy or Goodbye, Domini Investments CEO Carole Laible joins Jared Blikre to take a closer look at why she's bullish on energy infrastructure developer Quanta Services (PWR) and bearish around software company Wolters Kluwer (WKL.AS, WTKWY). To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

headline: Is Comfort Systems Scaling Modular Capacity for the Data Center Boom?
summary: FIX is expanding modular capacity to meet late-cycle data center demand, targeting 4M sq ft by 2026 as backlog stretches revenue into 2027-2028.

headline: Should You Buy FIX After a 6-Month Surge and Record Backlog?
summary: FIX has surged for six months after a blowout quarter and record $11.94B backlog, but with shares near a high-30s forward P/E, future gains hinge on execution.

headline: MasTec's Backlog Up by $4.7B: Sign of Sustained Infrastructure Demand?
summary: MTZ's backlog jumps to $18.96B. Does this surge signal a sustained infrastructure supercycle across energy, grid and data centers?

headline: Has Quanta Services (PWR) Run Too Far After Infrastructure And Energy Transition Tailwinds?
summary: If you are wondering whether Quanta Services' share price still reflects fair value after a strong run, you are not alone. The stock closed at US$549.22, with a 7 day return of a 2.8% decline, a 30 day return of 12.4%, a year to date return of 24.9%, a 1 year return of 128.3% and a 3 year return of 243.5%. Over 5 years the return is very large. Recent news flow around Quanta Services has focused on its role in large scale infrastructure and energy transition projects, along with ongoing...
